MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-17-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. BRUCE J. HIGHMAN OF THE LAW FIRM OF HIGHMAN, HIGHMAN & BALL, TEL. NO. 982-5563 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. SEJAL THAKKAR OF THE MANNING & MARDER LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. (619) 515-0269 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT AND MOVING PARTY.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT CONTEMPORARY SERVICES CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS RAISED TRIABLE ISSUES OF MATERIAL FACT AT LEAST AS TO PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS FOR HARRASSMENT AND RETALIATION GIVEN UNWELCOME FLIRTATIONS AND CONTACTS AND 35 SEXUALLY SUGGESTIVE MESSAGES FROM HIS SUPERIOR CONTINUING AFTER HE ASKED HER TO STOP AND AFTER SHE SUGGESTED TO HIM THAT COMPLAINTS COULD LEAD TO DISMISSAL, ESPECIALLY GIVEN EVIDENCE THAT THE CONDUCT WAS SUFFICIENT TO CAUSE PLAINTIFF TO RESIGN AND THAT PLAINTIFF'S JOBS WERE REDUCED AFTER HE COMPLAINED TO HIS SUPERVISOR. Prevailing party is to submit a proposed form of order pursuant to CCP 437 C(G). Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR #8189 =(301/PJB)

Off Calendar Mar-18-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
SEJAL THAKKAR, ESQ., (415)217-6990, FOR DEFENDANT/OPPOSING PARTY
NO APPEARANCE WAS MADE BY THE MOVING PARTY

THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF VITTORIO FRONCILLO'S MOTION For Leave To Complete Discovery Closer To Trial Date Or To Reopen Discovery After New Trial Date Is Set. THE COURT GRANTS PLAINTIFF LEAVE TO CONDUCT FURTHER DISCOVERY BETWEEN NOW AND APRIL 1, 2010, SPECIFICALLY, TO DEPOSE JERRY MASTERSON, THE TWO WITNESSES TO THE SEXUAL HARASSMENT OF WHICH JERRY MASTERSON COMPLAINED, CHRIS ARMSTRONG, AND CLARA MUHLENBRUCH. JUDGE: WALLACE P. DOUGLASS. MATTER NOT REPORTED (WPD/612)
